title: Can Learning a Foreign Language Delay Dementia
subtitle: 学外语能防痴呆吗？科学证据与常见误解

paragraphs.en: You may have heard that learning another language is one method for preventing or at least postponing the onset of dementia. Dementia refers to the loss of cognitive abilities, and one of its most common forms is Alzheimer's disease. At this time, the causes of the disease are not well understood, and consequently, there are no proven steps that people can take to prevent it. Nonetheless, some researchers have suggested that learning a foreign language might help delay the onset of dementia.
paragraphs.zh: 你可能听说过，学习另一种语言是预防或至少延缓痴呆症发作的方法之一。痴呆症指认知能力的丧失，其最常见的形式之一是阿尔茨海默病。目前，人们对该病的病因尚不完全清楚，因此还没有经过证实的预防措施。尽管如此，一些研究人员仍提出，学习外语可能有助于延缓痴呆症的发作。

paragraphs.en: To explore this possibility more deeply, let's look at some of the common misconceptions about dementia and the aging brain. First of all, dementia is not an inevitable part of the normal aging process. Most older adults do not develop Alzheimer's disease or other forms of dementia. It is also important to remember that dementia is not the same thing as normal forgetfulness. At any age, we might experience difficulty finding the exact word we want or have trouble remembering the name of the person we just met.
paragraphs.zh: 为了更深入地探讨这一可能性，让我们先看看关于痴呆症和大脑衰老的一些常见误解。首先，痴呆症并非正常衰老过程中不可避免的一部分。大多数老年人并不会患上阿尔茨海默病或其他形式的痴呆症。同样重要的是要记住，痴呆症与正常的健忘不是一回事。在任何年龄，我们都可能一时想不起确切的词语，或者记不住刚认识的人的名字。

paragraphs.en: People with dementia have more serious problems, like feeling confused or getting lost in a familiar place. Think of it this way: If you forget where you parked your car at the mall, that's normal; if you forget how to drive a car, that may be a signal that something more serious is going on. The idea that dementia can be prevented is based on the comparison of the brain to a muscle.
paragraphs.zh: 痴呆症患者则有更严重的问题，例如感到困惑或在熟悉的地方迷路。可以这样想：如果你忘了在商场把车停在了哪里，那是正常的；如果你忘记了如何开车，那可能就预示着更严重的问题。认为痴呆症可以预防的观点，是基于将大脑比作肌肉的类比。

paragraphs.en: When people talk about the brain, they sometimes say things like "It is important to exercise your brain" or "To stay mentally fit, you have to give your brain a workout." Although these are colorful analogies, in reality the brain is not a muscle. Unlike muscles, the brain is always active and works even during periods of rest and sleep. In addition, although some muscle cells have a lifespan of only a few days, brain cells last a lifetime. Not only that, but it has been shown that new brain cells are being created throughout one's lifespan.
paragraphs.zh: 当人们谈论大脑时，有时会说“锻炼大脑很重要”或“要保持大脑健康，就得让它锻炼”。尽管这些都是生动的类比，但实际上大脑并不是肌肉。与肌肉不同，大脑始终活跃，即使在休息和睡眠期间也在工作。此外，部分肌肉细胞的寿命只有几天，而脑细胞却能持续一生。不仅如此，研究还表明，人的一生中都会不断产生新的脑细胞。

paragraphs.en: While it makes for a colorful analogy, comparing the brain to a muscle is inaccurate and misleading. So, if the brain is not a muscle, can it still be exercised? Once again, researchers don't know for sure. There are now many computer, online, and mobile device applications that claim to be able to "train your brain," and they typically tap into a variety of cognitive abilities.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管将大脑比作肌肉是个生动的类比，但这既不准确也有误导性。那么，如果大脑不是肌肉，它还能被锻炼吗？研究人员对此仍无定论。如今有许多电脑、在线及移动设备应用程序声称能够“训练你的大脑”，它们通常涉及多种认知能力的运用。

paragraphs.en: However, research suggests that although this type of training may improve one's abilities at the tasks themselves, they don't seem to improve other abilities. In other words, practicing a letter-detection task will, over time, improve your letter-detection skills, but it will not necessarily enhance your other perceptual abilities. However, there is some reason to believe that learning languages might be different.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，研究表明，尽管这类训练可能提高人们在特定任务上的能力，但它们似乎并不能提升其他能力。换句话说，练习字母识别任务确实会逐渐提高你的字母识别技能，但不一定能增强你的其他感知能力。不过，有理由相信学习语言或许是个例外。

paragraphs.en: The best evidence that foreign language learning confers cognitive benefits comes from research with those who are already bilingual. Bilingualism most commonly occurs when children are exposed to two languages, either in the home or more formally in early schooling. But bilingualism certainly occurs in adulthood as well. Bilingualism and multilingualism are actually more common than you might think.
paragraphs.zh: 外语学习能带来认知益处的最佳证据，来自对已掌握双语者的研究。双语现象最常见于儿童接触两种语言的环境，无论是在家中还是在早期学校教育中更为正式地习得。但双语能力当然也可能在成年期形成。双语和多语现象实际上比人们想象的要普遍得多。

paragraphs.en: In fact, it has been estimated that there are fewer monolingual speakers in the world than bilinguals and multilinguals. Although in many countries most inhabitants share just one language, other countries have several official languages. Switzerland, for example, has four official languages: German, French, Italian, and Romansh. Throughout large parts of Africa, Arabic, Swahili, French, and English are often known and used by individuals who speak a different, native language in their home than they do in the marketplace.
paragraphs.zh: 事实上，据估计世界上说单语的人口少于说双语或多语的人口。尽管在许多国家，多数居民只共用一种语言，但其他国家则有多种官方语言。例如，瑞士有四种官方语言：德语、法语、意大利语和罗曼什语。在非洲大部分地区，许多人除了在家中使用一种母语外，在市场交易中还会知晓并使用阿拉伯语、斯瓦希里语、法语和英语。

paragraphs.en: So bilingualism and multilingualism are to be found throughout the world. And with regard to cognitive abilities, the research on those who speak more than one language paints an encouraging picture. For one thing, bilinguals are better at multitasking. One explanation of this superiority is that speakers of two languages are continually inhibiting one of their languages, and this process of inhibition confers general cognitive benefits to other activities.
paragraphs.zh: 因此，双语和多语现象遍及全球。而在认知能力方面，对掌握一种以上语言者的研究描绘出一幅令人鼓舞的图景。首先，双语者更擅长多任务处理。对这种优势的一种解释是，使用两种语言的人需要持续抑制其中一种语言，而这种抑制过程为其他活动带来了普遍的认知益处。

paragraphs.en: In fact, bilingual individuals outperform their monolingual counterparts on a variety of cognitive tasks, such as following complex instructions, and switching to new instructions. For the sake of completeness, it should be noted that the advantages of being bilingual are not universal across all cognitive domains. Bilingual individuals have been shown to have smaller vocabularies and to take longer in retrieving words from memory when compared to monolinguals. In the long run, however, the cognitive and linguistic advantages of being bilingual far outweigh these two drawbacks.
paragraphs.zh: 事实上，双语个体在执行复杂指令以及切换到新指令等多种认知任务上，表现均优于单语者。为求完整，应该指出的是，双语的优势并非遍及所有认知领域。与单语者相比，双语者的词汇量较小，从记忆中提取单词的速度也更慢。然而，从长远来看，掌握双语的认知和语言优势远远超过这两个缺点。

paragraphs.en: If the benefits of being bilingual spill over to other aspects of cognition, then we would expect to see a lower incidence of Alzheimer's disease in bilinguals than in monolinguals, or at least a later onset of Alzheimer's for bilinguals. In fact, there is evidence to support this claim. The psychologist Ellen Bialystok and her colleagues obtained the histories of 184 individuals who had made use of a memory clinic in Toronto. For those who showed signs of dementia, the monolinguals in the sample had an average age of 71.4 years at time of onset. The bilinguals, in contrast, received their diagnosis at 75.5 years, on average.
paragraphs.zh: 如果双语的优势能够溢出到认知的其他方面，那么我们理应看到双语者患阿尔茨海默病的比例低于单语者，或者至少双语者发病更晚。事实上，确有证据支持这一说法。心理学家埃伦·比亚韦斯托克及其同事获取了184名曾就诊于多伦多某记忆诊所的个人的病史。在出现痴呆症状的患者中，样本中的单语者发病时平均年龄为71.4岁，而双语者被确诊时的平均年龄则为75.5岁。

paragraphs.en: In a study of this sort, a difference of four years is highly significant, and could not be explained by other systematic differences between the two groups. For example, the monolinguals reported, on average, a year and a half more schooling than their bilingual counterparts, so the effect was clearly not due to formal education. A separate study, conducted in India, found strikingly similar results: bilingual patients developed symptoms of dementia 4.5 years later than monolinguals, even after other potential factors, such as gender and occupation, were controlled for.
paragraphs.zh: 在此类研究中，四年的差异极为显著，且无法用两组之间的其他系统性差异来解释。例如，单语者平均报告的教育年限比双语者多一年半，因此这一效果显然不是正规教育造成的。另一项在印度进行的研究也得出了惊人相似的结果：即使控制了性别和职业等其他潜在因素，双语患者出现痴呆症状的时间仍比单语患者晚4.5年。

paragraphs.en: In addition, researchers have reported other positive effects of bilingualism for cognitive abilities in later life, even when the person acquired the language in adulthood. Crucially, Bialystok suggested that the positive benefits of being bilingual were only found in those who used both languages all the time. But encouraging as these kinds of studies are, they still have not established exactly how or why differences between bilinguals and monolinguals exist.
paragraphs.zh: 此外，研究人员还报告了双语对晚年认知能力的其他积极影响，即使语言是在成年后才习得的。关键的是，比亚韦斯托克指出，双语的积极益处仅见于那些持续使用两种语言的人。尽管此类研究令人鼓舞，但它们仍未能确切阐明双语者与单语者之间存在差异的方式或原因。

paragraphs.en: Because these studies looked back at the histories of people who were already bilingual, the results can only say that a difference between the two groups was found, but not why that difference occurred. Further research is needed to determine what caused the differences in age of onset between the two groups. Other studies of successful aging suggest that being connected to one's community and having plenty of social interaction is also important in delaying or even preventing the onset of dementia.
paragraphs.zh: 由于这些研究回顾的是已掌握双语者的历史，其结果只能表明发现了两组之间的差异，却无法解释为何会产生这种差异。需要进一步研究来确定是什么导致了这两组之间发病年龄的差异。其他关于成功老龄化的研究表明，与社区保持联系并进行充分的社交互动，对于延缓甚至预防痴呆症发作也很重要。

paragraphs.en: Once again, however, the results are far less clear than the popular media might lead you to believe. Older individuals who lead active social lives are, almost by definition, healthier than their counterparts who rarely leave their homes or interact with others. So we can't really say whether being socially active prevents the onset of dementia, or if people who don't have dementia are more likely to be socially active. But even if studying a foreign language is not a magical cure-all, there is one thing it will do: It will make you a better speaker of a foreign language. Doing that confers a whole host of advantages we do know about.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，这些结果再次远不如大众媒体让你相信的那般清晰明了。过着积极社交生活的老年人，几乎从定义上就比那些很少出门或与人交往的同龄人更健康。因此，我们其实无法确定究竟是社交活跃预防了痴呆症发作，还是没有患痴呆症的人更倾向于社交活跃。但即便学习外语并非神奇的万灵药，它也至少能做到一点：它会让你成为更好的外语使用者。而这一点，确能带来一系列我们已经知晓的益处。
